$schema.description: The URL of the schema file

public.description: Whether the documentation is publicly accessible by default

description.description: Brief overview of the project. Used for SEO and AEO.

anyOf.description: The logo (for both light and dark mode)

light.description: The file for the light version of the logo used in dark mode, including the file extension.

dark.description: The file for the dark version of the logo used in light mode, including the file extension.

href.description: The URL to redirect to when clicking the logo. If not provided, the logo will link to the homepage.

logo.description: The logo configuration. Can be a single image path for both light and dark mode, or separate paths for each mode with an optional click target URL.

anyOf.description: Path pointing to the favicon file in your docs folder, including the file extension. The favicon will automatically be resized to the appropriate sizes.

light.description: The file for the light version of the favicon used in dark mode, including the file extension.

dark.description: The file for the dark version of the favicon used in light mode, including the file extension.

anyOf.description: The path to the favicon. Can be a single file or a pair for light and dark mode.

favicon.description: The favicon configuration. Can be a single image path for both light and dark mode, or separate paths for each mode.

openapi.description: A string or an array of strings of absolute or relative urls pointing to the OpenAPI file(s)

asyncapi.description: A string or an array of strings of absolute or relative urls pointing to the AsyncAPI file(s)

expanded.description: The view mode of the API parameters. Defaults to `closed`.

post.description: OpenAPI spec field keys to render as post pills on every ParamField. For each key, the field value on the schema is surfaced as a pill (string → verbatim, boolean true → key name, array → one pill per element, object → skipped).

display.description: The display mode of the API playground. Defaults to `interactive`. Use `auth` to show the playground only to authenticated users.

proxy.description: Whether to pass API requests through a proxy server. Defaults to `true`.

credentials.description: Whether to include cookies/auth for cross-origin requests when proxy is disabled. Defaults to `false`.

defaults.description: Whether to show only required parameters in the examples, defaults to `all`.

languages.description: Example languages for the autogenerated API snippets

prefill.description: Whether to prefill the API playground with the examples

autogenerate.description: Whether to generate code samples for API endpoints. Defaults to `true`. If `false`, only manually-written code samples (from either `x-codeSamples` property in the OpenAPI spec or `<RequestExample>` components in the MDX files) will be shown.

examples.description: Configurations for the autogenerated API examples

url.description: The display mode of the base URL in the endpoint header. Set to `full` to always show the full base URL on every endpoint page.

mdx.description: Configurations for API pages generated from MDX files

api.description: API reference configuration and playground settings

default.description: The default light/dark mode. Defaults to `system`.

strict.description: Whether to hide the light / dark mode toggle. Defaults to `false`.

anyOf.description: Path or URL pointing to the background image file, including the file extension. Used for both light and dark mode.

light.description: The file for the light version of the background image used in dark mode, including the file extension.

dark.description: The file for the dark version of the background image used in light mode, including the file extension.

anyOf.description: The path to the background image. Can be a single file or a pair for light and dark mode.

image.description: The background image configuration. Can be a single image for both light and dark mode, or separate images for each mode.

decoration.description: The background decoration of the theme

light.description: The background color in light mode

dark.description: The background color in dark mode

prompt.description: The prompt to be displayed in the search bar placeholder

metatags.description: Meta tags added to every page. Must be a valid key-value pair.

indexing.description: Specify which pages to be indexed by search engines. Setting `navigable` indexes pages that are set in navigation, `all` indexes all pages. Defaults to `navigable`.

family.description: The font family name. Any [Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/) family works out of the box (e.g. `Inter`, `Open Sans`, `Playfair Display`). For a self-hosted font, set `source` and `format` as well.

weight.description: The font weight. Typically `400` for regular and `700` for bold. Variable fonts support precise weights like `550`.

source.description: URL or path to a self-hosted font file. Use this when the font is not on Google Fonts. Example: `/fonts/custom.woff2` or `https://cdn.example.com/font.woff2`.

format.description: File format of the self-hosted font. Required when `source` is set.

heading.description: Font settings applied to all headings (`h1`–`h6`).

body.description: Font settings applied to body text and the rest of the page.

fonts.description: Typography configuration. Use a single set of font settings for the whole site, or split into separate `heading` and `body` fonts.

library.description: The icon library used for all icon properties. Defaults to `fontawesome`.

eyebrows.description: The eyebrows style of the content. Defaults to `section`.

anyOf.description: Match the docs light/dark mode with `system`, or always use a dark theme with `dark`.

items.description: Path (absolute or relative) to a JSON file describing a custom Shiki language

anyOf.description: Pick a custom Shiki theme. Use a single theme for both modes, or set separate light/dark themes.

codeblocks.description: The codeblock theme configuration. Defaults to `system`. To use a built-in shiki theme, add the theme name as "theme" in the object. To use different shiki themes for light and dark modes, add "themes" in the object with "dark" and "light" keys.

banner.description: Display an announcement bar across the top of every page. When enabled, the banner is shown with the content you provide.

redirect.description: Whether to redirect to the home page, if the page is not found. When false, displays a 404 page.

title.description: The title of the error page

description.description: The description of the error page. Supports MDX formatting including links, bold, italic text, and custom components.

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

options.description: Quick actions surfaced in the page context menu, like copying the page contents or opening it in an AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, etc.)

display.description: Where to display the contextual options: `header` (default) shows them in the top-of-page context menu, `toc` shows them in the table of contents sidebar.

contextual.description: Context menu shown on each documentation page, providing quick actions for copying, viewing source, and opening the page in AI assistants and IDEs

appearance.description: Whether to render page thumbnails in light or dark mode. When unset, thumbnails are auto-generated from your theme colors.

background.description: Background image for the thumbnail. Can be an absolute URL or relative path.

family.description: The font family, such as "Open Sans", "Playfair Display"

fonts.description: The font to be used for thumbnails. Only family name is supported for Google Fonts.

drilldown.description: Whether to automatically navigate to the first page when navigation groups are expanded

interaction.description: Options for how users interact with links in the documentation

timestamp.description: When enabled, all pages will display the date the content was last modified. Default is `false`.

variables.description: Set of custom variables that can be re-used throughout the content

schema.description: Whether to include the full OpenAPI/AsyncAPI specification in API reference pages served as markdown to AI agents. Defaults to true.

instructions.description: Custom instructions appended to every page served as markdown to AI agents, in addition to Mintlify's default guidance. Use this to provide product-specific directions such as preferred CLIs, workflows, or interaction patterns. Provide a single string or an array of strings (rendered as separate lines).

markdown.description: Configuration for markdown pages served to AI agents

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

light.description: The color in hex format to use in light mode

dark.description: The color in hex format to use in dark mode

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

directory.description: Directory layout for this navigation node and its descendants. "accordion" and "card" show automatic directory UI on eligible root pages; "none" disables it. Inherits recursively; descendants can override.

href.description: A valid path or external link

openapi.description: A string or an array of strings of absolute or relative urls pointing to the OpenAPI file(s)

asyncapi.description: A string or an array of strings of absolute or relative urls pointing to the AsyncAPI file(s)

language.description: The name of the language in the ISO 639-1 format

default.description: Whether this language is the default language

content.description: The text content displayed in the banner. Supports basic MDX formatting including links, bold, and italic text. Custom components are not supported.

dismissible.description: Whether to show the dismiss button on the right side of the banner

type.description: The visual style of the banner. `info` uses the primary brand color for general announcements. `warning` uses an amber background for cautionary notices (maintenance, deprecations). `critical` uses a red background for urgent notices. Defaults to `info`.

color.description: Override the banner background color with a custom hex color. Accepts `{ light, dark }` for theme-aware colors. When set, takes precedence over the color implied by `type`. Banner text is white, so choose a dark enough background for legibility.

banner.description: Banner configuration specific to this language

socials.description: An object in which each key is the name of a social media platform, and each value is the url to your profile.

header.description: The header title of the column

items.description: The links to be displayed in the column

links.description: Enable columns with links to be displayed in the footer

footer.description: Footer configuration specific to this language

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

primary.description: The primary CTA in the navbar

navbar.description: Navbar configuration specific to this language

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

default.description: Whether this version is the default version

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

searchable.description: When true on a hidden tab or group, descendant pages remain indexed for search, sitemap, AI assistant, and llms.txt despite being hidden from the rendered navigation. Has no effect when hidden is false.

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

global.description: Add external links that will appear on all sections and pages irregardless of navigation nesting

boost.description: Search ranking multiplier for pages in this group (e.g. 3 = 3x boost)

icon.description: The icon to be displayed in the section

hidden.description: Whether the current option is default hidden

searchable.description: When true on a hidden tab or group, descendant pages remain indexed for search, sitemap, AI assistant, and llms.txt despite being hidden from the rendered navigation. Has no effect when hidden is false.

root.description: A page in the navigation. Referenced by the path to the page. Example: path/to/page.

expanded.description: Whether the group is expanded by default

pages.description: An array of page paths or groups

light.description: The light color. Used primary in dark mode.

dark.description: The dark color. Used primary in light mode.

colorsSchema.description: The colors to use in your documentation. At the very least, you must define the primary color. For example: { "colors": { "primary": "#ff0000" } }.

global.description: Add external links that will appear on all sections and pages irregardless of navigation nesting

navigationSchema.description: The navigation structure of the content

sourceRef.description: The mount path of the git source to inline

sourceRefSchema.description: Reference to a git source navigation section
